title: Operating Systems & Threading
description: Explore operating systems processes, threads, virtual memory, and concurrency primitives.
learningObjectives: Differentiate operating system processes from execution threads
Explain virtual memory pages and memory mapping
Identify concurrency problems like race conditions and deadlocks

sections.title: Processes, Threads, and Concurrency
sections.content: An Operating System manages hardware resources. A Process is an isolated running instance of a program with its own memory space. A Thread is a unit of execution within a process; multiple threads share the same process memory space. In game loops, offloading heavy calculations (like asset loading or pathfinding) to worker threads keeps the main render thread running smoothly.
